predicate.title: Fix token-usage transcript lookup for worktree-per-change sessions
predicate.problem: bindings/claude-code/usage.py's newest_transcript() hardcodes the session-transcript directory as ~/.claude/projects/-home-jerry-Repos, a value that predates the worktree-per-change convention. Every change since then lives under a different, worktree-qualified transcript directory, so token-usage evidence collection silently fails (no error, no CI hook) for 10 of 12 prior Change Records. This is finding 1 of roadmap-priority-2-metrics-integrity.
acceptance_criteria: newest_transcript() locates the correct session-transcript directory for the actual cwd the script is invoked from, computed dynamically rather than hardcoded
The directory-name derivation (cwd path with every / and . replaced by -) is documented inline so the convention is discoverable without re-deriving it
Missing-directory and missing-transcript failures raise clear, actionable SystemExit messages naming the path that was checked
